get.description: Returns the largest known fillable amount for a single deposit on the given source→destination route. Relayer-market layers return exact capacity; bridge-backed layers return a conservative probed lower bound.

schema.description: Unique identifier of the intent operation

parameters.name: full

schema.description: Whether to include intent operation details

parameters.description: Whether to include intent operation details

status.description: Overall intent status: PENDING, COMPLETED, or FAILED

type.description: Operation type: CLAIM, FILL, or BRIDGE_FILL

timestamp.description: Block timestamp in unix seconds (present when COMPLETED)

items.description: A single operation on a chain

operations.description: Operations grouped by chain. Each chain has one or more operations (e.g. CLAIM, FILL, BRIDGE_FILL).

createdAt.description: Intent creation time in unix seconds

latencyMs.description: Time from creation to the latest leg landing, in ms. Null until any leg lands.

settlementLayer.description: Settlement layer; null if the routing strategy is unrecognized

items.description: A token moved on this leg

tokens.description: All tokens moved on this leg

txHash.description: Transaction hash (present once the leg lands)

timestamp.description: Block timestamp in unix seconds (present once landed)

items.description: A single source or destination leg
A token moved on this leg

tokens.description: All tokens moved on this leg

txHash.description: Transaction hash (present once the leg lands)

timestamp.description: Block timestamp in unix seconds (present once landed)

destination.description: Destination (fill) leg; null before a fill is dispatched

chain.description: Chain ID the call executes on

phase.description: PRE_CLAIM runs on the origin chain before funds are claimed; DESTINATION runs on the destination chain after funds are delivered.

value.description: Native value sent with the call, in wei (base units)

items.description: A single call executed by the intent

executions.description: Calls the intent executes — preClaim ops on their origin chain and destination ops on the destination chain. The signed intended calls; no per-call result is recorded. Empty for token-only intents with no custom calls.

sponsored.description: Whether gas/fees were sponsored for this intent

sponsoredValue.description: Sponsored value in fee-token base units

protocolFee.description: Protocol fee in fee-token base units

cost.description: Cost summary from the recorded fee sponsorship. Amounts are raw base units; omitted when no sponsorship row exists.

details.description: Extended intent details, returned only when `full=true`

chainId.description: Chain ID where the token balance exists (CAIP-2)

address.description: Token contract address on this chain

decimals.description: Decimal places for this token on this chain. Sourced per-chain because the same logical token can have different on-chain decimals across chains (e.g. USDC: 6 on Ethereum, 18 on BNB Smart Chain).

amount.description: Token balance on this chain in the token's smallest unit

chains.description: Array of token balances per chain

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response (`pagination.nextCursor`). Omit for the first page.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response (`pagination.nextCursor`). Omit for the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max items per page (max 100)

fromChains.description: Source chain IDs — one per distinct chain the intent spends on

token.description: Intent value token. The token delivered on the destination chain, or the spent token for same-chain intents (which have no persisted delivery).

amount.description: Intent value in base units — delivered on the destination chain, or spent for same-chain intents (which have no persisted delivery; for same-chain swaps this is the input, not the received amount).

createdAt.description: Intent creation time in unix seconds

data.description: Intents on this page, newest first

nextCursor.description: Pass as `cursor` to fetch the next page; null when no more results.

post.description: Submits a quoted intent for execution. Takes the `intentId` from `POST /quotes` (`routes[].intentId`) plus signatures (origin, destination, optionally target-execution) and optional EIP-7702 authorizations.

intentId.description: Identifier of the intent returned by `POST /quotes` (as `routes[].intentId`).

targetExecution.description: Target execution signature (smart sessions only; omit for EOA)

chainId.description: Chain ID for EIP-7702 delegation, 0 means it can be applied to any chain

address.description: Address of the delegate for EIP-7702 delegation

sponsor.description: EIP-7702 authorizations signed by the sponsor account. Each entry `chainId` is a CAIP-2 string (e.g. `eip155:8453`); the `0` any-chain sentinel is sent as a number.

chainId.description: Chain ID for EIP-7702 delegation, 0 means it can be applied to any chain

address.description: Address of the delegate for EIP-7702 delegation

recipient.description: EIP-7702 authorizations signed by the recipient account. Each entry `chainId` is a CAIP-2 string (e.g. `eip155:8453`); the `0` any-chain sentinel is sent as a number.

parameters.name: x-api-key

intents.description: Array of intents, each mapping token addresses to amounts that can be filled by a single relayer

post.description: Splits token amounts into multiple intents based on available relayer liquidity. Each returned intent can be filled by a single relayer.

chainId.description: The chain ID the intents settle on (CAIP-2)

tokens.description: Map of token addresses to amounts

settlementLayers.description: Which settlement layers the orchestrator may use. `{ include: [...] }` (allow-list) or `{ exclude: [...] }` (deny-list, inverted against the orchestrator's live layer set); a bare array means `include`. Internal modes (`SAME_CHAIN`, `INTENT_EXECUTOR`) are not selectable. Default unset = all layers eligible.

parameters.name: x-api-version
parameters.description: API version. Required; pinned to this document.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: API key.

intentId.description: Server-stored intent identifier. Pass back to `POST /intents` to submit.

expiresAt.description: Quote expiry timestamp (Unix seconds). After this point, assume the quote is dead and re-quote.

seconds.description: Typical end-to-end fill time for this route in seconds. Directional, not guaranteed.

estimatedFillTime.description: Estimated fill time for the route

settlementLayer.description: Settlement layer selected for this route

types.description: EIP-712 type definitions keyed by type name

primaryType.description: Name of the top-level type to sign

message.description: Message values keyed by field name. uint256 fields are encoded as decimal strings on the wire and re-coerced to bigint client-side before signing.

items.description: EIP-712 typed data definition ready for `signTypedData`

origin.description: Typed data payloads for the origin legs, in submission order. Sign each entry and submit the signatures in matching order via `signatures.origin`.

types.description: EIP-712 type definitions keyed by type name

primaryType.description: Name of the top-level type to sign

message.description: Message values keyed by field name. uint256 fields are encoded as decimal strings on the wire and re-coerced to bigint client-side before signing.

destination.description: Typed data for the destination leg

types.description: EIP-712 type definitions keyed by type name

primaryType.description: Name of the top-level type to sign

message.description: Message values keyed by field name. uint256 fields are encoded as decimal strings on the wire and re-coerced to bigint client-side before signing.

targetExecution.description: Typed data for the target execution, smart sessions only. Omitted for EOA accounts.

signData.description: EIP-712 sign payloads the client submits back on `POST /intents`

chainId.description: Chain where this token leg settles

tokenAddress.description: ERC-20 contract address for this token

symbol.description: Token symbol. `null` when the internal token registry has no entry for this address.

decimals.description: Token decimals. `null` when the internal token registry has no entry for this address.

price.description: Unit price in USD. `null` when the price oracle has no data for this token.

amount.description: Token amount in the token's smallest unit

items.description: A single (chain, token) leg with amount, price, and metadata

input.description: Tokens debited from the user, coalesced by (chainId, tokenAddress)

chainId.description: Chain where this token leg settles (CAIP-2, any namespace)

tokenAddress.description: Contract address of the delivered token (EVM 0x or non-EVM base58)

symbol.description: Token symbol. `null` when the internal token registry has no entry for this address.

decimals.description: Token decimals. `null` when the internal token registry has no entry for this address.

price.description: Unit price in USD. `null` when the price oracle has no data for this token.

amount.description: Token amount in the token's smallest unit

items.description: A single (chain, token) leg with amount, price, and metadata

output.description: Tokens delivered to the recipient, coalesced by (chainId, tokenAddress)

total.description: Full route cost in USD, regardless of who pays. Equal to `sum(breakdown.*.usd)` modulo rounding.

usd.description: Total cost of this category in USD, regardless of who pays.

sponsored.description: True when a sponsor absorbs some or all of this category. The user-vs-sponsor split is not surfaced.

gas.description: Aggregate gas cost (destination fill, swap execution, origin gas)

usd.description: Total cost of this category in USD, regardless of who pays.

sponsored.description: True when a sponsor absorbs some or all of this category. The user-vs-sponsor split is not surfaced.

usd.description: Total cost of this category in USD, regardless of who pays.

sponsored.description: True when a sponsor absorbs some or all of this category. The user-vs-sponsor split is not surfaced.

usd.description: Total cost of this category in USD, regardless of who pays.

sponsored.description: True when a sponsor absorbs some or all of this category. The user-vs-sponsor split is not surfaced.

fees.description: Aggregate route fees with per-category breakdown

cost.description: Route cost: inputs, outputs, and fee breakdown

type.description: Discriminator: `approval` means the user must ERC-20 approve `spender` for at least `amount` before submission.

amount.description: Minimum required allowance, in the token's smallest unit.

spender.description: Always the canonical Permit2 contract on this chain. The EOA approves Permit2; the settlement contract pulls funds via Permit2 at claim time.

type.description: Discriminator: `wrap` means the user must wrap native ETH into the chain's WETH-equivalent before submission.

amount.description: Minimum amount to wrap, in wei.

tokenRequirements.description: Pre-flight token operations the user must perform before submitting this route (approvals, wrapping). Emitted for EOA accounts only — smart accounts handle these internally.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

requestId.description: Relay.link request ID. Use against Relay.link's status API to track the destination-chain fill.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

depositAddress.description: NEAR Intents deposit address. Track fill status via the NEAR Intents status API keyed on this address.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

commitmentId.description: Rhino.fi commitment ID. Use against Rhino.fi's status API to track the destination-chain fill.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

sourceDomainId.description: Circle CCTP source domain ID — needed to fetch the attestation for the burn message on the source chain.

bridgeFill.description: Provider handle for resolving the destination-chain delivery transaction when settlement hands off to a third-party bridge (RHINO, RELAY, NEAR, CCTP, OFT). Absent when the orchestrator settles end-to-end (e.g. ACROSS, ECO).

routes.description: Route candidates ranked by the orchestrator's internal scoring (cheaper + faster wins). The first entry is the recommended route — most clients should submit it without inspecting the rest.

schema.description: Response body for `POST /quotes` on the blanc API version

post.description: Computes a quote for an intent: token transfers, target executions, and cost breakdown. Returns a pre-ranked `routes[]` with per-route `intentId` to pass to `POST /intents` for execution.

destinationChainId.description: CAIP-2 chain identifier of the destination chain (EVM `eip155:<id>`, or non-EVM `solana:…`/`tron:…`)

tokenAddress.description: The address of the requested token. Format depends on destination chain — 0x-hex for EVM, base58 SPL mint for Solana, T-address for Tron.

amount.description: The amount of the requested token (in the smallest unit). Omit for max-out.

balance.description: HyperCore balance class for this token request — 'spot' or 'perp'. Optional; defaults to 'perp' when destinationChainId is HyperCore. Rejected on every other destination.

tokenRequests.description: A list of token requested on the target chain

accountType.description: Account type. Blanc clients should use `ERC7579`; the legacy `smartAccount` value is equivalent and accepted for alps wire compatibility, and gets rewritten to `ERC7579` by the `alpsAccountType` versioning change before Zod validation.

setupOps.description: Setup operations for the smart account. Only used if the account is not deployed

mockSignatures.description: Per-chain stub signatures used only during gas estimation. Required for smart-session flows where the real signature length depends on session policy — the orchestrator needs a same-shape placeholder to simulate the verification cost. Keys are chain IDs as decimal strings; `"0"` is a cross-chain fallback applied when no chain-specific entry matches.

mockSignature.description: Deprecated. Must be omitted; use `mockSignatures` (keyed by chain id) instead.

contract.description: address of contract to which delegation on behalf of sponsor will be assumed for given chain ID

delegations.description: Per-chain specific map to delegated contract address for 7702 delegations. Use `0` to indicate cross-chain delegation

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

destinationExecutions.description: Execution calls on the target chain.

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

preClaimExecutions.description: Execution calls to perform before the claim on each origin chain, keyed by chain ID. Max 10 ops per chain, max 5 chains.

destinationGasLimit.description: The gas limit for the target chain executions

accountAccessList.description: Account access list specifying which chains and tokens an account may access

address.description: Recipient address. Format depends on destination chain — 0x-hex for EVM destinations, base58 for Solana, T-address for Tron.

accountType.description: Account type. Blanc clients should use `ERC7579`; the legacy `smartAccount` value is equivalent and accepted for alps wire compatibility, and gets rewritten to `ERC7579` by the `alpsAccountType` versioning change before Zod validation.

setupOps.description: Setup operations for the smart account. Only used if the account is not deployed

mockSignatures.description: Per-chain stub signatures used only during gas estimation. Required for smart-session flows where the real signature length depends on session policy — the orchestrator needs a same-shape placeholder to simulate the verification cost. Keys are chain IDs as decimal strings; `"0"` is a cross-chain fallback applied when no chain-specific entry matches.

mockSignature.description: Deprecated. Must be omitted; use `mockSignatures` (keyed by chain id) instead.

contract.description: address of contract to which delegation on behalf of sponsor will be assumed for given chain ID

delegations.description: Per-chain specific map to delegated contract address for 7702 delegations. Use `0` to indicate cross-chain delegation

settlementLayers.description: Which settlement layers the orchestrator may use. `{ include: [...] }` (allow-list) or `{ exclude: [...] }` (deny-list, inverted against the orchestrator's live layer set); a bare array means `include`. Internal modes (`SAME_CHAIN`, `INTENT_EXECUTOR`) are not selectable. Default unset = all layers eligible.

gas.description: Whether to sponsor gas for the intent

bridgeFees.description: Whether to sponsor bridge fees for the intent

swapFees.description: Whether to sponsor swap fees for the intent

signatureMode.description: How the user's intent signature will be verified onchain. `ECDSA` for plain EOA signatures; `ERC1271_EMISSARY` for smart-account signatures verified via TheCompact emissary delegation. The orchestrator picks a default based on `account.accountType` — only set this if you need to override.

feeToken.description: Reserved for future use. No effect today.

feeBps.description: App fee rate in basis points of the input value (0–10000 = 0–100%).

executionTokensReceived.description: Tokens that will be received by EOA executions. These will be swept to the recipient account.

auxiliaryFunds.description: Additional balances the quote should treat as available, beyond what is currently on the account. Use this to get a preliminary quote against funds you can produce by the time you submit — e.g. liquidity in a DeFi vault you will withdraw, an in-flight CEX deposit, or a parallel transfer from another wallet. Keyed by chain ID, then token address; amounts in the token's smallest unit.

selectionStrategy.description: How to rank candidate plans. `cheapest` minimizes direct USD cost. `fastest` minimizes estimated fill time (with cost tiebreaker). `best` balances both via a notional- and time-weighted shadow fee.

schema.description: Quote request. Chain ids are CAIP-2 strings (e.g. `eip155:8453`, `solana:…`, `tron:…`); bare numeric ids are rejected.
